source_jobs.title: Regional Manager
source_jobs.description:
At Planteria Group, we help organisations reimagine their workplaces, making them more productive, healthy, and sustainable. From creating warm, welcoming reception areas to designing large-scale living architecture, we turn ordinary spaces into vibrant places where people genuinely love to be.

We’ve grown from a small family business into the UK’s leading interior landscaping specialist, transforming workplaces and commercial environments with the power of nature, to international expansion with operations in the US. Along the way, we’ve partnered with some of the UK’s, and now US’s most recognisable brands to bring biophilic design to life, from lush office greenery and elegant corporate floristry to spectacular roof gardens and living walls.

Our success is driven by a fantastic team of 185 employees internationally, and we’re continuing to expand. We’re now looking for passionate individuals to join us on this journey and help us shape the future of sustainable, inspiring workplace design.

 


YOUR IMPACT:

We are looking to bring onboard a Regional Manager to have ownership of our London East region. As our Regional Manager, you will be playing an integral part in ensuring the success of your region across key components, such as profitability, commercial success and the retention of our customers.

As a Regional Manager, your impact will stretch across effective leadership of your region, your team leader and wider service technician team. In this role, you who will oversee a team of circa 14 - 17 members and have an eye for ensuring your team is nurtured, performance is optimised and everyone is working together to achieve agreed key performance indicators.

An addition to the above, your impact will reach across:

 - Management and maintenance of Net Promoter Scores for your region.

 - Heightening of our clients experience through management of those relationships and being a core point of contract for them.

 - Delivering profitability across your region whilst have visibility of commercial elements such as contract reviews and pricing.

 - Managing resolutions and to ensure customer success.
   
    


YOU WILL BE A GREAT FIT IF YOU HAVE:

 - Proven experience managing teams (both directly and indirectly), with members based onsite and remotely.

 - Demonstrable experience in effectively managing client accounts, driving customer success, increasing customer engagement, and building strong, long‑term client relationships.

 - Experience managing B2B clients who are end‑users across any industry and/or Facility Management organisations.

 - A genuine desire to support and develop your team, with a nurturing and coaching‑led approach.

 - A strong sense of ownership in your work, with the ability to assess situations and make data‑driven decisions aligned to business priorities and customer expectations.

 - Comfortable working in environments with shifting priorities and able to maintain a strong bias for action.

 - A desire to exceed customer expectations through the quality of your work and service delivery.

 - Experience in managing P&L budgets.

Please note, that this is a field-based role attending our client sites within the designated London region (With some occasional travel to our HQ in Henham, Bishop's Stortford).

 

At Planteria Group, our culture is guided by our core values: Humble & Confident, Positive & Energetic, One TEAM, Relentless to Improve, and Create Wow!

If you want to be part of a flourishing, fast-growing business that values agility, collaboration, and creativity, we’d love to hear from you. If you feel that you don’t quite hit the experience, we’re after, please do apply anyway as we’re always looking for people who are looking to grow and develop us!

Planteria Group is proud to promote equality of opportunity and an inclusive culture where everyone can thrive.

---

Accessibility & Adjustments

We want every candidate to have the best possible experience throughout our recruitment

source_jobs.title: Christmas Coordinator - 6-Month Fixed Term Contract
source_jobs.description:
At Planteria Group, we help organisations reimagine their workplaces, making them more productive, healthy, and sustainable. From creating warm, welcoming reception areas to designing large-scale living architecture, we turn ordinary spaces into vibrant places where people genuinely love to be.

We’ve grown from a small family business into the UK’s leading interior landscaping specialist, transforming workplaces and commercial environments with the power of nature, to international expansion with operations in the US. Along the way, we’ve partnered with some of the UKs, and now US’s most recognisable brands to bring biophilic design to life, from lush office greenery and elegant corporate floristry to spectacular roof gardens and living walls.

Our success is driven by a fantastic team of 185 employees internationally, and we’re continuing to expand. We’re now looking for passionate individuals to join us on this journey and help us shape the future of sustainable, inspiring workplace design.




YOUR IMPACT:

As our Christmas Coordinator, you’ll play a pivotal role in bringing festive magic to workplaces, hospitality venues and commercial environments across the UK. You’ll be at the heart of our Christmas operation, coordinating the delivery of stunning seasonal displays while ensuring every client receives an exceptional experience from enquiry through to installation and collection.

Key responsibilities include:

 - Acting as the primary point of contact for incoming Christmas enquiries from our Business Development team and external channels.

 - Preparing and issuing accurate quotations and proposals, ensuring a fast turnaround and exceptional attention to detail.

 - Coordinating subcontractors for installations nationwide, including communicating schedules, client requirements and project expectations.

 - Issuing Risk Assessments and Method Statements (RAMS) and ensuring all necessary project documentation is in place.

 - Managing the dedicated Christmas inbox, ensuring all enquiries and communications are responded to promptly and professionally.

 - Handling incoming calls from clients, prospects and subcontractors, delivering outstanding customer service at all times.

 - Maintaining accurate records and project information across internal systems and CRM platforms.

 - Managing projects involving Christmas trees, wreaths, garlands and bespoke festive decoration schemes.

 - Contributing to the creation of memorable festive environments that enhance workplaces and create a welcoming seasonal atmosphere.




YOU WILL BE A GREAT FIT IF YOU HAVE:

 - Strong verbal and written communication skills, with the confidence to engage professionally with clients, subcontractors and colleagues.

 - A proactive, energetic approach and enjoy working in a fast-paced, customer-focused environment.

 - Excellent attention to detail and take pride in delivering accurate work.

 - Strong organisational skills with the ability to manage multiple tasks, projects and deadlines simultaneously.

 - A collaborative mindset and enjoy building positive working relationships with a variety of stakeholders.

 - A bias for action, responding quickly to enquiries and driving tasks through to completion.

 - A strong sense of ownership and accountability for your work.

 - A solution-focused attitude and a passion for delivering great customer experiences.

 - The ability to thrive in a structured and process-driven environment, particularly during busy seasonal periods.



At Planteria Group, our culture is guided by our core values: Humble & Confident, Positive & Energetic, One TEAM, Relentless to Improve, and Create Wow!

If you want to be part of a flourishing, fast-growing business that values agility, collaboration, and creativity, we’d love to hear from you. If you feel that you don’t quite hit the experience, we’re after, please do apply anyway as we’re always looking for people who are looking to grow and develop us!

Planteria Group i

source_jobs.title: HR Advisor / Generalist
source_jobs.description:
At Planteria Group, we help organisations reimagine their workplaces, making them more productive, healthy, and sustainable. From creating warm, welcoming reception areas to designing large-scale living architecture, we turn ordinary spaces into vibrant places where people genuinely love to be.

We’ve grown from a small family business into the UK’s leading interior landscaping specialist, transforming workplaces and commercial environments with the power of nature, to international expansion with operations in the US. Along the way, we’ve partnered with some of the UKs, and now US’s most recognisable brands to bring biophilic design to life, from lush office greenery and elegant corporate floristry to spectacular roof gardens and living walls.

Our success is driven by a fantastic team of 185 employees internationally, and we’re continuing to expand. We’re now looking for passionate individuals to join us on this journey and help us shape the future of sustainable, inspiring workplace design.




YOUR IMPACT:

You’ll play a key role in shaping a smooth, engaging employee journey while supporting managers and driving people excellence across the business.

 - Deliver a seamless onboarding experience - coordinate pre-employment checks, vetting, and inductions in partnership with L&D and hiring managers

 - Be a trusted advisor to managers - provide first-line guidance on people queries and support employee relations cases (disciplinary, grievance, absence, performance) alongside the HR Business Partner

 - Build strong relationships - develop a deep understanding of teams to spot trends, challenges, and opportunities early

 - Use data to drive decisions - analyse people metrics (attrition, engagement, performance) and turn insights into meaningful action

 - Keep HR operations running smoothly - maintain HRIS data, manage employee lifecycle changes, and oversee benefits and admin processes with accuracy and efficiency

 - Continuously improve processes - identify opportunities to streamline workflows and reduce manual or inefficient ways of working

 - Partner with Talent Acquisition - ensure a smooth, high-quality post-offer journey through to day one and beyond

 - Enhance employee experience - contribute to engagement initiatives, survey follow-ups, and ongoing improvements

Success in this role looks like:

 - High-quality, timely delivery of HR support

 - Strong stakeholder satisfaction and trust

 - Effective resolution of employee relations matters

 - Clear, actionable insights that drive positive change




YOU WILL BE A GREAT FIT IF YOU HAVE:

 - Strong organisational skills - able to plan, prioritise and manage multiple tasks with ease

 - Excellent communication - clear, empathetic, and able to influence and guide stakeholders confidently

 - A proactive, solutions-led mindset - you don’t just spot problems, you solve them, balancing people needs with business outcomes

 - Sound judgement under pressure - calm, professional, and focused when handling sensitive situations

 - High attention to detail - delivering accurate, high-quality work consistently

 - A confidential and professional approach - trusted with sensitive information and situations

 - Solid understanding of UK employment law and HR best practice

 - Problem-solving capability - able to think critically and make informed decisions quickly

Experience & Qualifications:

 - CIPD Level 3 (or equivalent experience)

 - Previous experience in a HR Generalist, Administrator, or similar role

 - Experience using HR systems and managing employee data and documentation

 - Practical knowledge of applying employment law and implementing changes

 - Experience using people data (e.g. engagement, attrition, performance) to inform decisions

 - Exposure to People/HR initiatives such as engagement, culture, or organisational change

 - Experience working in fast-paced or evolving environments, with the ability to improve or build processes

Tech & Tools:

 - Exp

notes: No tech roles posted; ops-heavy with focus on interior landscaping and workplace transformation
